risk_if_not_implemented: Without External Connectivity Requirements - Identification of Ports, Protocols & Services, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Third-Party Services
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to mitigate the risks associated with third-party access to the organization's Technology Assets, Applications, Services and/or Data (TAASD).
compensating_control_1.justification: Third-Party Services (TPM-04) provides third-party oversight that compensates for the absence of External Connectivity Requirements - Identification of Ports, Protocols & Services (TPM-04.2) by extending security obligations contractually and monitoring third-party risk in lieu of direct control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the identification and implementation of relevant statutory, regulatory and contractual controls.
compensating_control_2.justification: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance (CPL-01) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of External Connectivity Requirements - Identification of Ports, Protocols & Services (TPM-04.2) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
